_meta.description: Consolidated reportable metrics for the Schwarzschild QNM project. ALL Zerilli numbers are transcribed verbatim from paper/sections/results.tex (which paper/main.tex \input's) -- the single authoritative source. The Regge-Wheeler block is a local verified run. The slides block is quarantined and NON-authoritative.
_meta.authoritative_source: paper/sections/results.tex (== paper/main.tex). This file takes precedence over slides, memory, and any prior transcription.
_meta.units: Geometric units, M=1. M_omega = M*Re(omega); tau_over_M = tau/M. Percentage errors are |extracted - theory|/theory * 100.

provenance_legend.results_tex: Transcribed verbatim from paper/sections/results.tex tables/equations (authoritative; part of main.tex).
provenance_legend.main_tex_abstract: From paper/main.tex abstract prose (authoritative but rounded).
provenance_legend.this_run_local: Computed locally in this workspace and verified (exact float values from saved JSON).
provenance_legend.slides: presentation/slides.tex (model in base repo PC_Physics_Inspired_Neural_Network). AUTHORITATIVE alongside main.tex per project lead. Holds the faithful Zerilli reproduction + the RAD-sampling ablation.
provenance_legend.chat_memory_post_HPC_loss: Kerr Teukolsky results reconstructed from Copilot chat history + notes/results_record_2026-06-30.md after the CSD3 HPC (which held all models/corpora/report.json) went offline. LOWEST confidence: NOT independently reproduced in this clone; code re-implemented + only synthetically validated.

_meta.reproducibility: The paper aggregator scripts/aggregate_results.py rebuilds outputs/reportable_results.json from the per-run outputs/ JSONs (HPC-only, not in this clone). Keys here mirror the results.tex tables so values can be cross-checked.
_meta.paper_structure: Section 1 = faithful reproduction of Patel (2024) for BOTH potentials at base config (uniform, 15k L-BFGS): zerilli_l2_faithful_reproduction + regge_wheeler_l2_faithful_reproduction, each compared to patel2024_baseline. Sections 2+ = our extensions (forward_pinn greedy+30k, inverse, curriculum, fno, hybrid), reported for ZERILLI ONLY -- justified by isospectrality (RW and Zerilli share the QNM spectrum, so the QNM-targeting extensions give equivalent conclusions on the axial potential).
_meta.updated_2026_07_03: Added hybrid colab_reproduction_2026_07_03 block: Colab-trained model, canonical sweep (xq 2-20), window-geometry t=100 experiment. Numbers verified by local inference + FD solves.

truth.source: Leaver 1985 continued-fraction Schwarzschild l=2 fundamental; Berti, Cardoso & Starinets 2009 Table II

patel2024_baseline.provenance: patel2024 (paper.txt Table 3 QNM for both potentials; Zerilli field RL2/MAD via results_tex Table 2)
patel2024_baseline.note: Target-paper reported values, ell=2. Patel's QNM reference: tau=11.241 M, omega=0.374/M (note: they round omega to 0.374, we use 0.3737 -- their % errors are vs 0.374). Table 3: FD1/FD2 = FFT+envelope / curve-fit on FD; PINN1/PINN2 = same on PINN. Percentages are Patel's reported % errors.

shared_hyperparameters.note: Architecture + training config shared by the forward PINN and all 6 inverse variants. Forward/inverse differ only in L-BFGS iters (30k/15k) + inverse-specific blocks.

forward_pinn_zerilli_l2.config_note: Enhanced: architecture-matched Patel baseline + doubled L-BFGS (15k->30k) + residual-greedy adaptive sampling (0.3*Nr on high-residual band). This IS the paper's forward result.

esprit_m3_decomposition.note: Full K=6 ESPRIT decomposition of the forward PINN field on [10,50]M (appendix tab:esprit-fwd). All |A_k| are >=20 orders below the signal (~1e-1); decomposition is in the numerical-noise floor. M3 selects largest-amplitude (k=6, spurious); (omega,tau)-closest to Leaver is k=5.

inverse_pinn_zerilli_l2.setup: Joint training of network field, mass M, and single-mode ringdown template against Nobs=500 noisy field samples at 1% Gaussian noise; 10k Adam + 15k L-BFGS. omega from M4 plateau; tau from post-hoc M2-wide on the converged field (avoids A_ring-tau template degeneracy). Best = variant D (run zerilli_l2_inverse_qnm_combo).

variants_summary.columns: RL2_pct, M_pct_err, M_omega_M4, sigma_omega, omega_pct_err, tau_over_M_M2wide, tau_pct_err

B.flag: two-mode NLS did not converge; M4 undefined

variant_definitions.note: appendix tab:inv-hyper. Common: M_init=0.8, (omega,tau,A_ring,phi)_init=(0.30,8.0,0.5,0.0), lambda_data=10, Nobs=500, sigma_noise=0.01. Varying columns below.

all_variants_all_methods.note: appendix tab:m1m2-all + tab:m4m5-all: M1/M2/M4/M5 for forward + all 6 inverse variants. M4/M5 carry sigma_omega. Minor rounding differences vs the main-text summary tables; appendix is the fuller set.

curriculum_pinn_zerilli_l2.setup: Temporal domain split into windows trained sequentially with numerical-IC transfer; per-window Adam 10k + L-BFGS 30k. Compared against single-window forward baseline.
curriculum_pinn_zerilli_l2.note: W1=[0,25]M reaches RMSD 5.16e-4, a 37% improvement on the time-restricted baseline RMSD (8.2e-4) over the same sub-domain, but full-domain RMSD does not improve (IC-transfer interpolation error compounds per splice).

fno_operator_zerilli_l2.setup: Amortised 2D FNO (~7.8e7 params) trained once on 1000 (M,x0,sigma) draws (M in [0.8,1.2], x0 in [-10,30], sigma in [3,8]); 300 epochs Adam cosine, no L-BFGS. Extraction primarily at xq=2M.

qnm_extraction_xq2.columns: FNO omega_pct_err, FNO tau_pct_err, FD omega_pct_err, FD tau_pct_err

population_100bh_median_xq2.columns: omega_pct_err_median, tau_pct_err_median, n_omega, n_tau, pass1(<1% omega)

population_field_rmsd_by_bin.note: appendix tab:fno-pop-heatmap: v4 late-time field RMSD sqrt(<(Phi_FNO-Phi_FD)^2>)_{t>T/2} across the 100-BH test set, binned per initial-data axis. ~3x band across the whole box; no isolated failure region.

version_ablation.columns: tmax_over_M, modes_t, modes_x, lambda_ring, lambda_obs, lambda_tw, canonical_field_RMSD, canonical_omega_pct_err, pop_omega_pct_err, pop_tau_pct_err

zero_shot_resolution_transfer.note: v4 trained at 401x401, evaluated at 5 grids with no retraining; canonical BH; M3 ESPRIT omega at xq=2M.

cost_A100_hours.note: FNO ~10 A100-h train (one-time, 300 epochs) + ~3e-4 h/BH inference; PINN ~1.5 A100-h/BH. Break-even ~7 BH.

hybrid_surrogate_zerilli_l2.setup: 1.13e6-param 2D FNO learns the discretisation residual of a weak k=4 coarse FD solve (coarse dx,dt=(0.8,0.4)M; 1/64 fine cost) against a label-free Richardson-extrapolated target, with a parameter-free gradient gate (scale s=1e-3, the 68th percentile of |grad prior|). k=4 prior RL2 = 4.93%.

qnm_extraction_xq2_median_100bh.columns: hybrid omega%, hybrid tau%, coarse-up omega%, coarse-up tau%, fine-FD omega%, fine-FD tau%

gate_scale_sweep.note: appendix tab:gate-scale-sweep: label-free re-gating of the single trained model; canonical-BH field error vs gate scale s. Deployed s=1e-3 is within 2% of the global-RMSD optimum (s~1.5e-3). Columns: smooth-cell RMS (bottom 60% gradient), wavefront-cell RMS (top 40%), global RMSD.

hybrid_surrogate_zerilli_l2.honest_finding: At xq=2M the coarse-up baseline already sits at the plateau-extractor floor; the hybrid's value is the GLOBAL field (79x population MSE, 10.1x canonical RMSD), NOT the single-observer QNM.

colab_reproduction_2026_07_03.note: Independent end-to-end reproduction on Colab A100-40GB; corpus bit-identical to local Sobol draws; artifacts installed at outputs/hybrid/fno_sw_gate_s1em3.

zerilli_l2_faithful_reproduction.config: configs/zerilli_l2_paper.yaml (uniform resampling, Adam 10k + L-BFGS 15k; Patel settings)
zerilli_l2_faithful_reproduction.note: Faithful reproduction of Patel for the even-parity (Zerilli) potential at base config (no greedy/RAD/extra iters). Model trained in base repo PC_Physics_Inspired_Neural_Network; numbers from presentation/slides.tex (authoritative). QNM methods: M1 = FFT+envelope, M2 = damped-cosine curve fit (Patel's two methods). Compare to patel2024_baseline.qnm_table3.zerilli.

regge_wheeler_l2_faithful_reproduction.config_note: FAITHFUL paper reproduction (uniform resampling, Adam 10k + L-BFGS 15k, NO greedy/RAD, NO extra iters). Odd-parity (axial) potential; pairs with zerilli_l2_faithful_reproduction for the reproduction section. Isospectral with Zerilli. Compare to patel2024_baseline.qnm_table3.regge_wheeler.

qnm_extraction.note: All five methods (M1-M5) + M2-on-M4-plateau, matching the paper's Zerilli coverage. M3 ESPRIT is ill-conditioned (|amp|~1e-24) and returns garbage, exactly as for the paper's forward Zerilli field -- diagnostic only.

kerr_teukolsky_hybrid_NONreproduced.WARNING: Kerr Teukolsky s=-2 hybrid (coarse-FD + FNO residual). Computed on the CSD3 HPC, now LOST; reconstructed from chat history + notes/results_record_2026-06-30.md. NOT in main.tex. NOT reproduced in this clone (corpus/model lost; code re-implemented + synthetically validated only). Lowest confidence -- re-run locally before reporting.
kerr_teukolsky_hybrid_NONreproduced.setup: Hyperboloidal minimal-gauge Teukolsky s=-2, sigma in [0,1]; ell=4, m=2, M=1. Decoupled label-free design: model input = N=101 order-2 coarse prior (k8, keeps QNM headroom); target = order-4 Richardson (16*u401 - u201)/15 (both rungs 4th order); no fine solve in any label.

best_run_decoupled_label_free.corpus: phase_c_l4_decoupled: ell=4; Sobol a/M in [0,0.95], r0 in [8,11], w in [1,1.5]; train 1024 / val 128 / test 128; grids fine=801, k2=401(o4), k4=201(o4), k8=101(o2 prior); float32 storage.

test_medians_by_spin.note: median % errors over 128-sample held-out test set; field prior->hybrid; Mw/tau prior->hybrid, fine-field floor in _fine.

gates.note: field 0.78%<<5% PASS; Mw median 1.16%>1% FAIL (dragged by low-spin bin where fine itself only 2.46% = extraction-floor regime).

best_run_decoupled_label_free.floor_theory: target field 0.026% << hybrid 0.78% => model hit its input-limited floor (~0.77%), did not saturate the cheap target.

three_way_comparison.note: field / Mw(high spin) / tau median % err. Point: decoupled order-4 Richardson matches supervised, label-free, at ~0.32x fine cost.

decoupled_order4_richardson.flag: BEST: matches supervised on field+Mw, label-free

tau_weakness_diagnosis.status: diagnosed; envelope-normalization fix implemented + synthetically validated locally (kerr/src/hybrid_data_pipe.py norm_mode=envelope), but NOT trained on a real corpus.
tau_weakness_diagnosis.root_cause: dynamic range, not noise: late ringdown is clean learnable signal (Richardson label matches fine to 0.0-0.2% there) but amplitude-weighted L2 + single peak-dominated scale s=rms(|prior|) give it ~1e-10 of the loss.

ell2_phase_c.qnm: net-negative (ensemble prior 0.119% -> hybrid 0.510%): prior already at QNM floor, no headroom at ell=2

ell4_n101_order2_richardson.outcome: FAIL (student saturated bad order-2 target); motivated the order-4 fix

ell4_n101_supervised.outcome: dual-win but uses fine labels (replaced by decoupled run)

rad_sampling_ablation_zerilli_l2.note: AUTHORITATIVE (presentation/slides.tex). Extension exploration: residual-adaptive distribution (RAD) sampling sweep on the Zerilli base model (15k L-BFGS). Motivated the residual-greedy adaptive sampling in the final forward extension (forward_pinn_zerilli_l2). p_i ~ |r_i|^k + c. This is an EXTENSION, distinct from the faithful reproduction (zerilli_l2_faithful_reproduction) and from the final forward result (greedy+30k, RL2 0.58%).
